description:
The Breakdown of Mass is the next installment for System Vector, which is becoming a favorite game of mine in general. 
The expansion does not focus much on a large amount of new units or system cards. Instead, it adds more depth to the game it's self by adding more elements to game play and strategy. The expansion includes 6 new high-resource system cards, a Black Hole system, and an Unknown system. 
In addition to these, the game also features a new building aspect, where you can build structures such as factories, science institutes, and mobile shipyards. Each of these structures improves different aspects of your empire, making the games focus wider and your empire larger. With these buildings also come the chance to research and develop new technology and advancements in your empire. There will be a military technology tier, and an economical technology tier. These tiers will have 2 options per level of advancement. This will give players 4 Research Management Cards and 48 Research counters (12 for each player).

That makes 13 new cards to print out, which at 25 cents a print, comes to $3.25.

8 System cards (including the Black Hole and Unknown System)
4 Research Management cards
1 Research Counters sheet

I am really excited to be getting another System Vector game off the ground. I am lucky to be able to pop things like this out within a couple of days, while still being able to test play Armed Legion AND learn/play RftG. fun fun.

boardgame: Space War I.: The Burning Stars
description:
Space War is a fast playing Space Combat and Strategy Game. Two Players (in the Basic Version and up to 12 in the extended Versions) You have the Choice to conquer the Space, to travel with their Ships in the Hyperspace and Attack the Enemy wherever they can. Building of Ships, Bases and Conquering Planets with Landing Troops, is the Target of the Game. But to Attack your Enemies Planets you must defeat the Fleets of his Force first. The winner is who has cleared the Space from its Enemy... But be Carefull your Fleets didnt come exactley where you have sent it every time...

German Description:
Space War ist ein schnell spielbares Weltraumkampf und -strategiespiel für 2 bis 12 Spieler (Basisversion nur 2 Spieler).
Ihr habt die Wahl den Weltraum zu erobern! Eure Schiffe reisen durch den Hyperraum und greifen den Feind an wo immer Sie Ihn finden. Mächtige Sternenschiffe und Basen bauen, und die feindlichen Planeten mit Landungstruppen zu überrennen ist das Ziel. Doch um das zu erreichen müsst Ihr zuerst die Flotten der gegnersichen streitmächte schlagen. Der Sieger ist jener Spieler welcher zuerst die Galaxis von seinen Feinden gesäubert hat...

boardgame: Space War II.: The Road to Karaganda
description:
German Description:
Der Rat der Confederate Stars Administration (C.S.A.) gibt bekannt:
Um die Streitigkeiten wegen des, äußerst rohstoffreichen, Sonnensystems
KARAGANDA zu beenden findet ein Wettrennen dorthin statt. Jenes Sternenreich
welches zuerst einen von intelligenten Lebewesen bewohnten Planeten in das
umstrittene Sonnensystem bringt hat das Rennen gewonnen. Das Bombardement
der Planeten von Mitbewerbern ist verboten und wird mit Ausschluß aus dem
galaktischen Rat bestraft. Planeten von Mitbewerbern können jedoch erobert
werden wenn der eigene Planet durch Unfall oder Eroberung verlorengegangen ist.
Alle Planeten beginnen das Rennen am gleichen Tag.

Der Weg nach Karaganda... ist ein Weltraumstrategiespiel für 2 - 8 Spieler ab 12 Jahren.
Im Gegensatz zu "die brennenden Sterne" Wird Karaganda als Geschwaderkampf basierendes System gespielt. Natürlich können, alternativ auch die Raumgefechtsregeln davon verwendet werden!
Ihr müßt eure Welt ins Zentrum des Sektors, zum Karaganda
Sonnensystem bringen. Dabei sind verschiedene Hindernisse, wie Schwarze Löcher, Minenfelder, Asteroidenfelder, Dunkelwolken und Hyperstürme, zu überwinden.
Außerdem sind da noch eure Mitspieler die euch die Hölle heiß machen wo sie nur
können...

English Description:
The Council from the "Confederate Stars Administration" (C.S.A.) announces: To End The disputes about the, extremely raw material-rich, solar system KARAGANDA,  The race wich takes place there. That star realm which first places one planet inhabited from intelligent organisms, into that disputed solar system, has won. The bombardment of planets from competitors is forbidden and will be punished with exclusion from the galactic council. Planets of competitors can however conquered if the own planet by accident or conquest was lost. All planets begin the running on the same day. "The Road to Karaganda! is a space strategy game for 2 - 8 players starting from 12 years. Contrary to "The burning stars" Karaganda has a squadron fighting based system. Naturally, alternatively the space engagements rulesof the other Game can be used! You must bring your world in the center of the sector, to the Karaganda Solar system.Many Different obstacles, like black holes, Mine fields, Asteroidfields, dark clouds and hyperstorms must be overwhelmed. In addition your fellow players are making you the hell, where  ever they can...

Think Boggle with numbers, but instead of spelling out words, you're racing to put together equations. Tally Rally challenges math skills by requiring children to spot addition, subtraction, multiplication and division equations to win.

A simple word deduction game from the 1930s, produced as part of a series by Party Game Publishers (PGP) of Luton in Bedfordshire, England.

The games consist of 18 cards (games), each of which is printed with a black and white line drawing. Beneath the drawing is a cryptic clue to look for something in the drawing - for example, the drawing might be of two boxers fighting, and the cryptic clue might be, "spot the drink". The answer is "punch", which is something depicted in the drawing and which is also a drink.

The purpose of the game is for each player to see all 18 cards, and solve as many of the clues as they can within a set time.

Good production values - cards are heavy duty and well printed - but limited replay value, as once you have worked out the clues on each card there is no point in playing the game again as you know all of the answers. Simple pleasures for simple folk.

boardgame: ... aber bitte mit Sahne: Das Joker-Stück
description:
A mini-expansion for the game Piece o' Cake: the 'wild card' part of the pie has all the fruits depicted on it. The Joker is mixed with the other components. When the Joker is played during the game, it's submitted immediately to any variety that you collect OR you can eat it and get 2 points.

This expansion appeared in the April 2009 edition of Spielbox Magazine.

Ein Spiel für 2 - 6 Spieler um heiße Pizzen und um ebensolche Reifen...
Mach deinen Pizza-Express zum besten in der Stadt...

English Description:
Hot Pizza and hot Wheels...
drive your Pizza-Express delivery Service to the best in the City...

The Game is Based in this early Variant (Later Variants are in the Phase of Planing) on a Hex Grid with Numbers inside like Sttlers from catan. All Players are setting there Pizza Express Central on a field with a "2" marked in the Center. The Numbers represented the Amount of Fees you must pay to the owner of the Building (Landlord?) every Round. The Starting Player rolls two dice on the random Events Table. Then he rolls for the field where the Pizza Couriers, must deliver their Pizzas. Every Player has one starting Courier. The first one is roling a Die to determine the Number of Crossing Points, his Driver could move this Turn. After this he moves his first driver as Near as Possible to a field with a Number rolled as Target field. If he reachs a crossing point adjacent to the target field he is earning the Printed Number multiplied with 100. These are the Pizza Units or PU (PE in German).  But not PUs are the Target of the Game! The Victory depends on Victory Points based on Quality, Price and a Cutt throug rate of Delivery Times from the Pizza Orders.

I Personally like this Game but the realy simple Grafics of the Game had once avoided any succes on the Market. So i decided to release the Game with better Artwork in 2010 hopefully...

No English rules Available yet but in Praparation Status!

Produced by Waddingtons in the 1920's.

Two games in one; a jigsaw board of the world, combined with a general knowledge game where players have to correctly identify the locations of capitals of the world. However, the general knowledge part of the game cannot be played until the jigsaw board has been completed.

The jigsaw was 32" x 18", and at the time of production was the largest cardboard jigsaw ever made.

Game contents:

1 map of the world in the form of a 32" x 18" jigsaw
1 paper map of the world (as a reference for the jigsaw)
4 sets of capital city counters (red, blue, green and white)

In the general knowledge part of the game, each player in turn tries to correctly place a capital city on the jigsaw board.

If they correctly place the capital city, they score 4 points.
If they cannot place the capital city, they can put it to one side and score 0 points.
If they incorrectly place the capital city, they lose 2 points.

The winner is the player with the most points.

The game was something of a rarity even when in production; it was expensive, and the general knowledge part of the game was an obvious afterthought. However, it is prized by collectors of vintage jigsaws, and has some curiosity value for the now-obsolete names used for countries on the map - for example Siam, Persia, Arabia and Ceylon. Definitely a remnant of the British Empire ;)

A two-player expansion of the Sumeria board game, released as a promo for the game at Essen '09. It adds 24 more wooden pieces (6 in each of the original player colours) bringing the total to 20 per player. It also includes a sheet of rules and a sticker to show the 4th player turn which only happens in the 2-player game. The whole thing comes packaged in a baggie and cannot be played without the base game.

Expands:

    Sumeria

WAR OF BUGS is a step-by-step strategy board game for 2 or more players.

The main feature is that you can use everything to be the battlefield table, stairwell, park, living room and so on. Bugs will climb, jump, fly and fight amidst the landscape and stuff thrown around.
That's why the game is about bugs -they can get everywhere.

Now the game is absolutely free and works on «DOWNLOAD, PRINT & PLAY» principle.

* Players make moves by turn, trying to seize opponent's artifact
 * Bugs crawl, climb, fly, jump, fight, seize and defend bases and artifacts
 * Bases produce bugs

The goal is to defeat all opponents.
The player is defeated when the opponent's bug seizes his artifact.

Quarreling: the card game is a board game designed for couples and lovers to avoid their quarrels. Draw cards, play cards and count emotion points. He/She who gains the most points would win this game, but may also give u a sorry kiss. 
To my love Flaw.

Endemic pits two players controlling medical workers against a third player who controls a pair of diseases.

From the site:
"In a far away planet two diseases are starting to spread. A team has been called forth to fight the diseases off. But it seems the diseases have a mind of their own..."

description: Confugo (from Latin: "I take refuge") is a simple but addictive roll-and-move board game, suitable for players aged from 7 onwards. The objective is to move your pieces from one end of the board to the other whilst attacking your opponent's pieces and defending yourself.  In each move players have to decide whether to attack, defend or play safe. The winner is the first player to get all their pieces to safety.

Welcome to Detroit, Autobots! Your mission is to protect Earth from the evil Decepticons and other enemies. Travel through the city in search of enemies to defeat. Try to be the first Autobot to reach Sumdac's Tower and battle Megatron and you're the winner!

The train car you are on has broken away from the rest of the train, slowed to a stop, and now is heading back down the hill, towards the Train Station that's overlooking a canyon. The car is barely moving, but seems to be picking up speed, the smart thing to do would be to jump now...but, there still some time and the car is not moving that fast yet, you are sure you can toss off some your suitcases (with all your ill-gotten gains) before you have to jump. Too bad the other passengers seem to have the same idea.

The object of the game is to score the most points by getting  your suitcases off the train car. Suitcases have a point value from 1-7 points. There are also bonuses and score multipliers that can increase the points earned. You will at some point want to get your passenger off the train, because if the train does crash (which is very, very likely) you don't want to be on it.

Lightning McQueen, Chick Hicks and The King are dead even as they screech into the final lap of the Piston Cup! Who will take the trophy home? Play your cards right and it could be you!

Be the first player to score 10 checkered flag points by correctly guessing the order that the cars finish the race.

Bible Rack-O. Players attempt to arrange cards, each bearing the name of one of the books of the Bible, in their personal racks in biblical sequence. In standard Rack-O fashion, cards can be drawn from either the face down draw deck or the face up discard pile and must then be placed in the same slot from which the old card was removed. First to fill their rack in correct sequence wins.

Passion is a board game designed for lover's that want to have fun and spice things up by learning more about what each other want and like. You and your lover will be entertained for hours as you challenge each other for favor cards and try to stay away from penalty cards. Each step along the way will be a sensual experience and sexual exploration. Main goal is to gather points to win. And a winner will get...well who knows!!!

Games are a fun and healthy way to change things up a bit in your sex life. Pulling one out from under the bed every now and again can turn a night of love-making into a special romantic event.

This game includes 162 cards (Challenge, Favor, and Penalty), a game board, 2 tokens, a notepad, a blindfold, 2 spin dials, and 2 pencils.
France trivia game now has a junior version. Packed with 1500 fun questions that every child should know about the their country – music, sport, geography, nature, culture and much more

The travel version of the France Quiz. The game includes a total of 600 France concerning questions! By responding to the questions you tick off the pyramid in your subject areas. You be the first to check off 5 circles, you are a winner!

Here, the first to reach seven correct answers is the winner. Smart Games are different compared to other issues. For each correct answer you win a badge in your color. Here, the first to reach seven correct answers is the winner. Smart Games are different compared to other games like that. For each correct answer you win a badge in your color. Experience the laughter and excitement in the family's favorite issues. Here the issues 385 fun topics about anything and everything, from animals in Africa and subscribed magazines to mobile phones and planets. The questions fit to the whole family and also special issues for the youngest. Answer the questions in your favorite subject and try to win as much money as possible on your skills. When the timer starts ticking, you have 15 seconds for you to respond. While stressing players you through placing the chance card that means they probably know the answer and want to "steal" your question. The player with the most money at the game's final will be the family champion.

In this crazy and fun game, the player is, what animal and human faces tell about their personality. You may both comical male and female faces as different animal portraits. Players may turn put faces and read out the various weird claims like "Not sure about their sexual orientation." Since voting players individually which of the five faces that they think best meet this claim. Those who are in the majority may move up their game pieces on the board. The first player who will win in goal in this extremely entertaining game for adults.

2400 questions that interested community will love! Finally, ask a game that only raises the issue of what is important and interesting, namely the development in the Norwegian society after 1945. The new and exciting categories are tailored for those looking for a real challenge. The new categories are: Politics, Business, Norwegian ideas, Media, Norwegians abroad and Events and social development.

An interesting quiz game about the Norway - now in a handy travel size edition! The game includes 600 questions divided into six categories, which are History, Culture, Science, Goegraphy, Sport and Miscellaneous.

This popular trivia game now has a junior version. Packed with fun questions that every child should know about the their country – films, books, sport, fun places to visit and much more. Answer a question correctly from each category as you move around the board and try to collect tokens. The first player to collect 6 tokens wins.

An exciting and fun business games for the whole family where you can take risks in hazard games. If you like to buy and sell, your best bet is probably to obtain land and sites for building banks and casinos. If, on the other hand, you like to play on the casino, this may be your biggest source of income - you can be rich in a snap. Packed with fun questions that every child should know about the their country – films, books, sport, fun places to visit and much more. Do you dream about that being a horse riding beautiful Tina Lund? If so, the own game "Tina Lund World Cup", you get chance for that display your talents in the exciting game. To compete in the start of a borrowed horse and will be at collecting as many prizes together as possible, so to get your very own horse. description: Game "Lithuania" - all you need to know Lithuanian! In this game you will find interesting questions and answers on history, culture, nature, geography and sports themes. It is intriguing quiz, where you can play in teams or individually. Game "Lithuania" consists of six questions topics, 400 papers, 2400 questions with answers.

12 playing pieces with different symbols on front and back are laid out in 4 quartes of the playing area. One player takes the Hektor figure. The other players try to memorize the symbols (6 different). Then they close their eyes. “Hektor” flips one playing piece and the other players open their eyes again. Now they try to find out, which piece was flipped. The one who detects the piece first AND can tell which symbol is on the back, earns the piece nad will be the new “Hektor” for the next round.  A new piece is laid out. To win the game, you must collect 5 pieces.

Sediq Song（賽德克之歌）is a two-player postcard wargame and it simulates the Wushe Incident, October 27 to December 8, 1930.  Each turn represents one week and the map represents the Taichu Prefecture （台中州）.  One player commands the Japanese colonial forces（日本殖民政府）and the other player commands the Sediq warriors（賽德克族勇士）.  Japanese army will fail the battle if at least one Sediq warrior survives in Wushe division in the end of the sixth-turn.

It is the first postcard wargame of the Aboriginal Tribal Battles series designed by Formosa Force Games.

Two children play hide and seek in the garden of the house. The first to find the other is the winner.

A deducation game for two children. Each player takes a grid map of a garden, and play on either side of the screen. The gardens has a number of unique items in each column and row.

Taking turns, each players rolls two dice. One die shows the number of questions they may ask the other player to discern their location. The other die is the number of spaces the player may move their pawn.

When they suspect they are in the same square as the opponent, the player announces their location. If it is the same, the player wins.

"The 2000 year's game"

Game similar to connect 4, but played on a diamond shaped board of 11x10. The game ends when all the board is filled an then all the lines of 4 or more are scored. The player with the higher total score wins. 
Scoring:
4 in a row...1 point
5 in a row...2 points
6 in a row...3 points
etc...

El juego del año 2000

Juego similar a las 4 en raya, pero con tablero en forma de diamante de 11x10. El juego finaliza cuando se ha llenado todo el tablero y se puntúan todas las líneas de más de 4 fichas, ganando el jugador que más puntuación total ha conseguido.

La forma de puntuar es la siguiente:
4 en línea... 1 punto
5 en línea....2 puntos
6 en linea....3 puntos
etc.

The players are small animals trying to steal seeds from an angry woodpecker by hiding under the eggs in its nest.

Play starts by releasing the woodpecker. As it slides down towards the nest, players take turn rolling a die. Using the symbol revealed the players move one egg around the nest. Just be sure to remember which egg your animal is hiding under.

When the woodpecker reaches the nest, the player holding the die is caught and gets nothing that round. If the other players are alone in part of the nest, then they receive one seed.

The woodpecker is released again and a new round begins. This repeats until all the seeds have been stolen. The player with the most wins.

Based in the Asalto game, but using two joined boards. The players are attackers and defenders at the same time.

The objective of the game is to occupy the 9 spaces that defended the other player, or the destruction of his attack spaceships, or the immobilization of his deffense spaceships. In each turn the player can move an attack or deffense ship. The defense ships can move in all directions. Attack ships are limited to advance by the blue lines, not being able to use the green ones. The movement is realised square by square. The deffense ships can also eliminate the attacking ones like checkers. If they dont't do it then the deffense ship is retired from the board temporaly. A player can get back a deffense ship when he conquer a square of the last free line with his attack ship.


"Ataque y defensa con naves interplanetarias"Juego basado en el juego del Asalto, pero en el que se han unido dos tableros y los jugadores han de ser tanto atacantes como defensores.

El objetivo del juego es ocupar los 9 espacios que defendidos por el contrario, o por la destrucción sus naves de ataque, o la inmobilización de sus naves de defensa.

En cada turno el jugador podrá mover una nave de ataque o una de defensa. Las naves de defensa pueden mover en todas direcciones, mientras que las de ataque están limitadas a avanzar por las lineas azules, no pudiendo usar las verdes. El movimiento se realiza casilla a casilla, menos cuando una nave de defensa puede eliminar una nave atacante, haciéndolo como el el juego de las damas. Al igual que las damas el comer es obligatorio y si no se lleva a cabo la nave defensora puede ser "soplada". Las naves "sopladas" pueden reintegrarse al juego desde el centro del tablero si se ha conquistado alguna casilla de la última línea libre.

Klotrix Trivia is a promotional product for the Potassium Suppliment Klotrix. The game features general knowledge trivia questions and a special board with a track modeled after the patented fast release of Klotrix. On certain squares, players are allowed to answer an additional question to skip ahead on the track. The winner is the first person to make it back to their starting area.

Kirby the Board Game is a children's roll and move game based on the popular Nintendo video game character. The game features a double sided board with a different map on each side. Movement is determined by drawing numbered cards. One of the cards causes the player to lose their turn. The winner is the first player around the board.

The game was given away in Wendy's Kids' Meals.

description:
The Ticket to Ride Europa 1912 expansion for the Ticket to Ride series includes 101 Destination Tickets - the 46 original tickets, plus 55 new ones that enable 3 new variants to the Ticket to Ride Europe map: Europe Expanded, which adds 19 new routes to the original 46; Big Cities of Europe, with tickets to 9 major European cities; and Mega Europe, which uses all the new routes as well as the 46 original Destination Tickets.

This expansion also introduces Warehouses & Depots - new game rules and pieces that create an additional strategic layer and can be played with any of the Ticket to Ride maps. Wooden Train Depots are placed on cities selected by each player. Throughout the game, each player's Warehouse will accumulate Train cards and those who have the foresight, clever timing, or just plain good fortune to build a route to a Depot can make off with a fistful of Train cards. Depot placement and skillful Warehouse tactics become a key part of a winning Ticket to Ride strategy!

Part of Ticket to Ride series.
Simplified version of Scrabble Junior published on the back of a box of Cheez-Its.  Game is played using Cheez-Its  with letters on them as the tiles.

DEPRICATED ENTRY

    
    
         This entry is outdated. Entries for the individual booster packs now have their own entries.

    


Valkrill's Gambit is the tenth Small Expansion Set (wave 10) in the HeroScape series.  It is the first expansion that contained repainted figures, repackaged figures or rereleased figures.

There are 4 booster packs: 
Heroscape: Valkrill's Gambit – Champions of Renown Army Expansion - repackaged GenCon and Walmart exclusives
Heroscape: Valkrill's Gambit – Warriors of Feldspar Army Expansion - repaints of Dzu-Teh and Obsidian Guards
Heroscape: Valkrill's Gambit – Soulborgs Army Expansion - Soulborgs (rerelease of wave 5 booster pack)
Heroscape: Valkrill's Gambit – Warriors and Soulborgs Army Expansion - Warriors and Soulborgs (rerelease of wave 5 booster pack)

Each pack contains 4-7 figures and no glyphs.  The wave 5 rereleases contain terrain pieces.

Detailed list of figures

boardgame: Battleship: G.I. JOE The Rise of Cobra Edition
description: The battle between G.I. Joe™ and Cobra™ is about to begin! In this edition of BATTLESHIP, fall in with the heroic G.I. Joe team or suit up and fight for the cold-blooded Cobra. Call out coordinates and mark your hits and misses against your opponent. Once a fleet is destroyed, remove the mission sheet from the command unit to reveal the battlefield. For the first time ever, you’ll seek and destroy your enemy on land, at sea, and in the air- it’s the most versatile BATTLESHIP game yet!

A cross between what's recognized as the Windows Solitaire game (aka Klondike), dominoes, and Blink.

This is a single-player game where players lay down cards where the shorter width ends of cards touch the longer length sides of cards (Blink standard deck) and all touch only where there is at least one of number, color, or suite match.  Going off the edge of a table to match cards legally is a lost game, as is not being able to place a card next to other existing cards that match on at least one attribute for all edges that touch.

This game may take 40 minutes with a single deck.

Why do people climb rockfaces - because they are there and because they can.  Well you can if the conditions are right!

In Crag they are always right.  Crag is a solo dice and card game that tries to simulate some of experience of rock climbing.  A route is generated from deck of cards and then the player attempts to move up it.  The longer the route, the harder the moves the more tired they become.  Placing gear can help if you fall, but it won't stop you falling, and you only have so much gear.

The files for Crag can be found in the files section.

boardgame: Med Döderhultarn på världsomsegling till Blå Jungfrun
description:
"Döderhultarn" is the colloquial name for a Swedish artist (woodcarver) called Axel Petersson (1868-1925). "Blå Jungfrun" ("The Blue Maiden") is a small island outside of Sweden which is a nature reserve since 1926. Apparently Döderhultarn was a bit excentric, and also very fond of the island. This game tries to encompass all this in a story of how he travels all over the world before actually reaching Blå Jungfrun.

The game consists of a single sheet of paper, 76 x 54 cm, folded twice to create a thin booklet. Each space the players move through has a small illustration and a short verse, describing what happens.

The game is published to benefit "Svenska typografförbundets invalid- och tuberkulosfonder", the Swedish typographer union's invalid and tuberculosis funds.

Game is a unique adventure in Science and Fiction. It is an unforgettable journey of the first astronauts into Proxima Centauri - the closest start apart from the sun.
In the journey to Proxima Centauri - space ships will encounter different real and imagined situations, will meet U.F.O. and other civilizations.

This is a pretty simple game where one player aims to reach Proxima Centauri (rockets), while other one tries not to let it happen (UFO). On the top of it - there is a number of additional events in the space eventually - having impact on the game.

Game was designed originally in Poland and was never translated to other lanuages.
A simple Trivial Pursuit clone. All the questions are from the areas of politics and political history.

In The Puzzling Pyramid, each player builds a maze on the side of a pyramid for another to navigate, sight unseen. They then race each other to the top, turn by turn.

Four triangular pyramid faces sit atop a base. On the reverse of a player's pyramid face, you build a maze by placing a number of baffles between posts (at least one route must be provided, and handicapping can be achieved by limiting a player's number of baffles). The pyramid is then rotated a quarter turn so that each player faces a different side of the pyramid. On a player's turn, you use a magnetic wand on the surface to move a steel ball up through the maze on the back, toward the top of the pyramid. If the ball encounters a baffle, it's separated from the wand and drops to the bottom of the base. If a player is unsuccessful, the turn passes until on a subsequent turn someone successfully navigates to the top.

The Northern Guard is the main armed force of the North and leading the fight against the Southern MILICIA. It is an integrated army ready to take on all comers and hand them a resounding defeat. description:
Pop-A-Word is a word-forming dice game, self-contained in the familiar Kohner Pop-O-Matic mechanism.

On a player's turn, the tray containing nine letter cubes is popped and jiggled until lying flat in the center. The player then makes the highest-scoring word possible from the letters showing face-up, the score being the total of the point values on the letters used in the word. Letters cannot be used multiple times unless showing on multiple faces, and a blank can be used for any letter, though that letter scores 0. Bonuses of an extra turn and extra points are earned for words of 6 to 9 letters. The game continues until a player reaches 200 points or some other agreed-upon total.

There is an alternate game where all players form his or her own word simultaneously from the same pop.

Twenty-four square tiles are to be placed in a 5x5 tray (the center square is not playable); a player is eliminated when the player cannot make legal move; the last player to move, wins.

Each tile is a 2x2 square array of one of three colors - each permutation, ignoring rotation, is represented once among the tiles.

The game can be played solitaire as a puzzle.

Three new scenarios for Russia Besieged: Fall Blau (1942-1943), Zitadelle (1943-1944), and Downfall (1944-1945), plus a Turkey variant that explores the circumstances under which either Germany or Russia might have brought Turkey into the war on their side.

If this player doesn't have anything, you must give him an apple.

An innovative card game that breaks the conventional pattern of numbers and alphabets. the new Linkage links up the color dots with its own unique game theory. The game is developed to suit players of different ages and at different levels.

The new Linkage brings fun and join to everyone. And the game "Links" up your relationship with your friends and your family. Try the new Linkage -- you will like it!

Where high culture and pop culture collide... on your game table. This is a table-top implementation of Jellyvision's (now known as Jackbox Games) irreverent CD-ROM/online trivia game.

"Hosted" by wisecracking "Nate Shapiro" (game creator Harry Gottlieb), the game serves as a scorekeeper of dollar values won or lost answering sublimely-worded trivia questions. The game's hard-coded database tracks questions by code, dollar value, and correct answer. A question's code is entered, the question is read aloud by a player, and a sliding door reveals four multiple-choice answers. Players race to buzz in, either to answer or to "screw" an opponent by forcing the opponent to answer. The game reveals whether or not players answer correctly and adjusts scores accordingly. High score after ten questions wins.

Includes 125 question cards with 500 total general knowledge questions. At least two expansions were released--a TV Pack and a Movies Pack--each adding 113 cards with 450 (TV) or 452 (Movies) additional questions in the set's given subject. (More expansion sets may have been planned, but were not released.)

This game features a map of Europe across which players travel via train and sea.  They have a number of sea tickets, rail passes, and passports with which to complete the task.

Year published, 1920, is just a guess.

Shan Tu is a combination of Halma and Checkers that features a three-cornered board made up of a grid of circles, each connected with up to six adjacent circles.  (The board could easily be drawn with hexagons instead of circles and lines.)  The game is meant for three players but can be played by two.  Its pieces (ten per player) begin in one corner and move to an opponent's starting corner to be crowned, then must move to one of three central circles in order to win the game.  The first player to get one king to the central circles wins.  Pieces, be they ordinary un-promoted pieces or Kings, may move in any direction and capture opposing pieces by jumping.  Kings have no advantage other than that they are required to win the game.

According to The International Society for Board Game Studies, Shan Tu dates back at least to an 1895 advertisement describing it as "A worthy successor to Gobang, Reversi, Halma and other favourites."

This is a short and free expansion for the Song of Blades and Heroes skirmish wargame. It uses chess pieces and a chess board (alternative rules are included for using chess pieces on standard Song of Blades and Heroes terrain).

Each player commands the usual named pieces in chess: 1 King, 1 Queen, 2 Bishops, 2 Knights, and 2 Rooks. Before the game begins, the player has the opportunity to swap out named pieces for pawns (at the rate of 2 pawns per piece exchanged). Each playing piece has it's own special rules (all contained in the Song of Blades and Heroes rulebook, no other expansion is necessary).

The aim of the game is to take out your opponent's King by killing him or making him flee the board. Although chess pieces and board are used, this is not a chess variant - it is Song of Blades and Heroes to use with your chess set. The pieces retain the character of their equivalent pieces in chess, within the SBH setting.

The idea of Song of Rooks and Pawns is to offer a gateway introduction to the Song of Blades and Heroes system.

German Description: Kampf um die Sterne beruht auf dem erstmals 1986 fertiggestellten Vorläufer Space War. Bei dem Weltraumkampf und Strategiespiel Schicken die Spieler Ihre Flotten in epische Weltraumschlachten. "Die ersten Scoutschiffe sind von ihren langen, abenteuerlichen Reisen zurückgekehrt. Sie berichten von neuen, unerforschten Welten. Gleichzeitig verbreiten sie auch die Kunde, daß draußen, zwischen den Sternen, die schreckliche Furie des Krieges tobt.

Ein junges Imperium steht im Kampf gegen fünf miteinander verbündete Sternenreiche. Beide Seiten suchen Welten die sie entweder ausbeuten oder zu Verbündeten machen können.

Ohne daß die Menschheit es wollte wird auch sie in den Strudel des Verderbens hineingerissen.

So beginnt dann für die Menschheit der

KAMPF UM DIE STERNE

A. Das Basisspiel

KAMPF UM DIE STERNE ist ein Spiel für maximal sieben Personen ab 12 Jahren. Ziel des Spieles ist es entweder, nach einer vorher ausgemachten Anzahl von Spielrunden, die meisten Sonnensysteme (Variante A) oder mehr als die Hälfte aller Welten dieses Sternensektors (Variante B), zu beherrschen.

Alternativ können die Szenarien der Hintergrundgeschichte gespielt werden. Näheres dazu im Anhang (E).

2. Zeittafel

2051
Fünf Sternenvölker beherschen den, die Erde umgebenden, Raumsektor. Sie herrschen durch ihre Wirtschaftsmacht und mit Waffengewalt. Eine einzige unterdrückte, ausgebeutete Welt wagt sich zu wiedersetzen. Ihre einzigen Raumschiffe sind kleine wendige Raumjäger. Ihr Kampf ist "Gegen jede Chance".

2052 - 2082
Ein "Schrei nach Freiheit" breitet sich über die Welten des Sektors aus. Eine unterdrückte Welt nach der anderen schließt sich den Rebellen an. Diese Welten gründen die Konföderation von Konquerein.

Die fünf Sternenmächte gründen eine Allianz um gemeinsam gegen die Rebellen vorzugehen.

nachdem mehr als 1.500.000 Menschen von allierten Sklavenjägern entführt worden sind wird endlich eine wirkliche Weltföderation gegründet. Beginn der Aufstellung einer Strategischen Raumflotte unter dem neugegründeten Strategischen Raumverteidigungskommando.

Ihre Schwesterschiffe, die Columbus und die Vasco da Gama, brechen nur wenige Wochen später, nach Alpha Centauri und Proxima Centauri, auf.

Rückkehr der Magellan.

Eine Velkandrische Flotte ist unterwegs das "Ziel ERDE" zu erobern. Die beinahe unverteidigte Erde wird erobert.

"Die Hand des Schicksals" führt Henry St. George, einen von allierten Sklavenjägern entführten Terraner nach Konquerein. Er wird vom Rat von Konquerein mit der Aufgabe betraut der Erde ein Bündnis anzutragen. Die legendäre siebte Flotte von Konquerein stößt zur Erde vor und befreit sie von den Vilarii. 
Unter der Führung von Henry St. George wird Terra zum Verbündeten von Konquerein. "Die zwei Kreuze" von Konquerein und der Erde kämpfen nun gemeinsam gegen die Allianz.

2200

Die gesamten Streitkräfte der Allianz unternehmen mit allen Einheiten Ablenkungsangriffe entlang der ganzen Front. Quatrix is a two-player game with old world qualities. If you like Backgammon, this game is for you. You must use your best strategies along with the luck of the dice in order to win!

Rings and discs are used in Quatrix. When a disc and a ring are combined, they form a PiP (piece inside piece). Squeezing the ring with the disc inside allows the player to move both pieces together. There are two sets of PiPs in each color, seven pips in each set, and two sets of dice.

All the playing pieces are set up as PiPs on the starting tracks. Players will separate their PiPs as the game progresses. The first player to get his or her pieces reassembled as PiPs across the finish line of the same color wins the game.

Rings, discs, and PiPs can move horizontally and vertically backwards and forwards, but never diagonally. Pieces can jump rings and discs, but you may not jump over a PiP of the opposing color. The board is 7 squares wide by 11 squares long, and has a starting track on each end, and a finish line on the opposite side of the board six tracks away.

(From the instructions)

From the designer's Etsy listing (Etsy seller fun4thecar):

"This is a travel game I created after many trips with my nieces and members of our youth group. Simply roll the colored die. Match the color rolled on the die to the color on the chart and begin your search! Whoever finds the correct number of items first wins the round. Don't forget to shout "Score!" as you spot items along the road."

description:
What did all those laborers of the great Egyptian monuments do with their time off?  Just like us, they played games!

This exciting dice game is a race to the best score.  Will the ancient Atum bless you with many Scarabs or will you be plagued by the Scorpions of Hedetet?

Players roll the dice trying for special scoring combinations, but, just when you think you have a good score, another player rolls the Scorpion die!  Will you be Stung?

boardgame: Bob the Builder: Barrow Up Bob!
description:
2 players take turns placing tools onto Bob's wheelbarrow.  Eventually Bob decides he's had enough and will tip the lot onto the floor or earlier if the players are heavy handed. The player who causes it to fall gets penalty points.  The player with the least penalty points at the end of the game is the winner.

The game features a nice plastic model of Bob that holds the wheel barrow in place for children to place the tools. The wheel barrow itself features a mechanism that when triggered tips the barrow up and tools out.  Very much like Buckaroo!.

"For generations human teens have crept into pastures at night and tipped over defenseless cows in their sleep.

"Last night they tipped the wrong cow... Elsa was the prettiest in the pasture, and Clyde the Bull was in love.

"Seeking revenge, this morning Clyde rushed onto a nearby highway, causing an auto accident and stopping traffic for miles. Now he's organizing gangs of cows from the pasture to tip cars off the road.

"Watch out world, it's the cows doing the tipping now!"

Collect gangs of cows by drawing cards from the draw pile or your neighbors' discard piles. Use these gangs to tip over the 5 types of vehicle cards in 5 different colors. Your score is based on the set of vehicle cards you tip; Busses are worth more than Cars and Trucks, but they also require a bigger gang to tip over. Motorcycles aren't worth as much, but they're easier to tip (require a smaller gang).

You score for collecting types of vehicles as well as for collecting colors, so you can go for a bunch of Tractors, or you could try to collect all the Blue vehicles.

A card game simulating drivers in an apocalyptic future; where raiders and bandits roam the land, taking what they want by sheer force or through brute intimidation.  The players are wanderers; drivers of cars that have been modified to help them endure the perils “out there” as they travel the highways between fortified villages and settlements.

The game can be played by one or more players. Ideally, the game is intended to be played by “more” players but works fine as a solo game. In a multi-player game, the individuals can work together, or against each, or as teams if they have enough players.

The basic component is a standard deck of 52 cards, a token to show the drivers movement across the cards, and two 1d6 to keep track of the drivers two stats: health/speed.

Each player is trying to get his driver across a wasteland of broken roads, and marauding bandits.  The cards themselves are used as the road, modifiers during movement, and combat against marauders or other players.

The card suite and face value will determine the type and strength of encounters the driver will face along the road,  The driver will in turn use their hand of cards to control the speed of their vehicle as well as the strength of their attack and defense.   During a co-op game, the drivers can use their cards to help friendly drivers fight off marauders or control their driving reactions.


The game was designed as part of the Traditional Card Game design contest.

'Bobry' is a simple children's game. It has two variants. One of them - 'Swimmers race' - is a racing game. In the other one - 'Building beaver's lodge' - players collect tokens with elements depicted on them. The first player to collect four different elements of the construction wins the game.

description:
Are you ready to lead your Legionnaires?!

According to the Gamealogical Institute History Department (wink, wink), this strategy game is derived from teaching methods used in the development of new Centurion Candidates in the far reaches of the north-west during the Late Roman empire.

Protect your flanks, master the use of the column and square formations and prove to the Training cadre that you are ready to lead.  This is a 2 player strategy game with very dynamic play; particularly as columns and square formations can break up and new ones be formed as you direct individual units on the battle field.

Tacticum includes multiple play setups known as "training scenarios".  Additional scenarios are added to the Gamealogical Institute website.

The simulation is designed for two players, one with a Muslim fleet and one with a Christian fleet.
Both players should be aware that although they primarily represent admirals with dreams of defeating the whole enemy fleet, they also represent subordinate officers who have personal objectives. They must allow for the wishes of individual captains to seek plunder on wrecked ships before their rivals get to it.

19 Oct – 26 Nov 1965
A conflict simulation re‐enacting the battles of October and November 1965 in the Central Highlands of VietNam.

"Plei Me 1965" is a two player wargame simulation recreating the actions of the US 1st Cavalry Division (airmobile) in the Pleiku province of South Vietnam during October and November 1965. The NVA B3 Front had an audacious plan to split South Vietnam in two with a strike through the central highlands, if it wasn’t for the intervention of America’s latest formation in airmobile warfare they could have very well succeeded. The Campaign had many varying types of force engagement; sieges, ambushes, running battles and whole unit encirclements. 
The simulation is meant to reflect the uncertainties in jungle fighting and the rapidly changing situations involved with airmobile operations.

description:
This is a solitaire simulation of the Second Lebanon War 2006. The player represents the Israeli supreme political leadership (i.e. the Prime Minister and the War Cabinet) contested by the designed system, representing Hezbullah.

     The player’s aim is to defend his home-front from rockets fired at it by Hezbullah, and to maintain and improve his political conditions, domestically and internationally, towards the end of the game.
     The player must consider various factors that affects the campaign’s outcome: domestic and international political factors, military mobilisation and operational successes and losses.
     The player will keep good record of points on two designated tables: domestic political points and international political points. A third table will be used to keep track of turns. Each turn ends only after playing all phases.
     The initial setting of the simulation represents the first stage of the conflict. Therefore, the player has thirty-three days of war to simulate in eleven turns.

Afghanistan 1980 is a strategic simulation of the fight between the Mujahideen and the Armed Forces of the Soviet Union in the unforgiving mountainous terrain of Afghanistan.
The simulation begins in early 1980 after the initial occupation force of the 40th Army has been deployed.

description:
From the Inside Cover:

this is a set of variants and games based on Diplomacy, the popular game of international negotiation, intrigue, and warfare (voted Game of the Year in 1976 and 1977 by Games & Puzzles readers).  It is necessary to know how to play Diplomacy in order to use this booklet.  Many of the games also require use of a Diplomacy board and pieces.  Mapsheets are provided for others, but a set of pieces is still required.  In all cases all additional rules needed are included.  The games range from the abstract to World War II to "swords and sorcery", some for fewer than seven players.  They are arranged in a progressive instructive format to enable inexperienced players to learn simple variants first and more complex ones later.  There are also special sections on two - and three - player Diplomacy.

Table of Contents :

Part I Simple Variants

  Colonization

Part II Moderate Variants

3 11x17 mapsheets are included

In the World of ChessCraft, there are 4 races. Each race represented by a different colour. Orc is Red, Undead is Black, Human is Blue and Elf is Green. Each has their own unique abilities to influence the game play. Orc has piece promotion and piece destruction. Undead has graveyard manipulation and piece control. Human has piece position and board manipulation. Elf has card manipulation. The battle has just began!

ChessCraft is an abstract game played with cards, for 1 vs 1 or 2 vs 2 players. Each game takes around 30 min to 1 hr. Contents of the full games are:
a)    32 pieces
b)    75 cards
c)    16 board tiles

The objectives of the game are 
(1) Capture the opponent king 
(2) Accumulate 20 or more cards per player 
(3) When the king reaches the opponent’s summoning board tile

Players take turns to place the board tiles. At the beginning of each player's turn, he draws 1 card from a common draw pile. During his turn, the player has to make a move on one of his pieces. At any time during his turn, he can 
(1) Summon basic or advanced pieces 
(2) Promote basic pieces to advanced pieces 
(3) Play cards

To do these 3 actions, costs must be paid first, which means discarding cards equal to the cost face down to a common discard pile. Whenever a card is played, the opponent may respond by playing a card. Cards are resolved first in last out. During opponent's turn, player may also play cards. Player can also modify the movement, capture or interaction with opposing piece of a piece in play by paying the cost of a Piece Modifier Action card. The effects of the modification lasts only the turn the card is played. Unsummoned pieces are in each player’s reserve. When the pieces are captured (or destroyed), they are placed in each player’s graveyard.

There are 2 types of cards. Actions go to the discard pile face up immediately while Permanents stay in play in front of the player who played them. Kings cannot be targeted or affected by cards unless specified. Player may not Check the opponent if he plays any card prior to his Move.

Each 2x2 board tile has a coloured circle in one of the squares. The circle indicates the square as a terrain of that colour. At the beginning of a player’s turn, if his pieces occupy all 4 terrains of his own colour, he can draw 1 more card.

Recicle, in portuguese.
Recycle is a strategy game about the cycle of organic and recyclable materials in a city.
Each player commands a team of street Collectors (four Collectors for two and three players, three Collectors for four players). They must move and collect materials and take them to the Cooperative. Each player also run a Cooperative business that buy the materials from the teams of Collectors. The Cooperative has a factory of recycled products and a trash yard for organic materials. The Cooperative pays the collecting costs and may hold up to three different equipments to make products from each of the five recyclable materials (paper, glass, long-life/tetra-pak, plastic and metal). 
At the end of every turn, the Cooperative sells their products obtaining qualifying points and Pratas, the money of the game.
When the trash yard is full of materials the game ends. Wins the game the player who gets more qualifying points.

boardgame: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
description:
Tiger's electronic Who Wants to be a Millionaire? table-top game brings all the excitement of the hit TV quiz show into your home.  One to six players compete for £1,000,000.  Lights, music, sound effects, and Chris Tarrant's voice add to the drama.  Just like on TV, you have 3 lifelines - Fifty-fifty, Ask the Audience, & Phone-A-Friend - to get a little help!

description: Luftsturm is a supplement for Luftwaffe 1946 (required to use it) and comprises 40 pages, plus 4 pull-out card sheets of extra aircraft stats. Roughly half of the book is background, outlining the structure of the Luftsturm and the tactics planned for the various "what if" aircraft featured, while the other half is additional rules, and 10 scenarios that are the real core of the book. These range from small and historical (a Sunderland vs 3 Ju-88s over Biscay) to massive and hypothetical (54 Soviet aircraft pressing home a bombing raid against jet fighters, Ba-349 Natters, and even Zeppelin Rammers....)

Amerika Bomber is the second supplement to the Luftwaffe 1946: Fight for the Skies game.

"The Americans think they are safe behind their oceans.  They think they can rain fire and destruction upon our people with impunity.  It is time for them to reap what they have sown.  This wonder weapon, forged by the fearless workers of the Reich, will teach the reckless Americans that no one is beyond our reach..." - Adolf Hitler at the christening of the first AR-555 Amerika Bomber

The book includes alternate history and characters from the Luftwaffe 1946.  Additional rules for: Air to Surface Missiles, Ship Statistic Generation.  10 Mission scenarios, 2 Mini-Campaigns and 48 Aircraft Stat Cards.

52pp, black and white, color covers.

It's snap with a twist, don't match the pairs but listen to the sound and then snap a card that matches.

Includes songs, sounds and characters from 6 hit shows: Thomas and friends, Bob the builder, Pingu, Barney, Angelina Ballerina and Fireman Sam.

The cards containing pictures of favourite characters are spread evenly across the floor.  Children then use sticks (or fly swats is a better term) with rubber suckers on the end to pick up the cards when they hear the relevant characters theme tune or catch phrases.

Publisher Blurb:

At last - a thrilling TWO-PLAYER, two-book Fighting Fantasy adventure!*

The perilous Trial of Kingship awaits you! In the golden city of Gundobad, you are twin princes - one Warrior-Prince and one a Warlock-Prince - each with your own particular skills and strengths. It is time for one of you to succeed to the throne, and you must face the Trial of Kingship. But only one of you can win through. Which brother will it be?

Beware! Vile monsters and deadly dangers lie in wait. Two dice, a pencil, an eraser - and a friend! - are all you need.


    Each book can also be played solo!


Set contains: 
Clash of the Princes - The Warlock's Way
Clash of the Princes - The Warrior's Way

Gotham is a tile-laying game set around the turn of the twentieth century.  Players take turns laying tiles to complete city blocks, which they can them claim for points.  More residences on each block means more points.  And civic improvements -- such as churches and schools -- can boost the value of a block.  But watch out:  homes can only be built where commerce supports it.  And urban blights -- like jails and slums -- can reduce a profitable city block into a landlord's nightmare.

This is a free print and play game.  It uses graphics from old insurance maps, and produces a lovely tabletop!

boardgame: Star Wars: Destroy Death Star Game
description:
Based on the game box photos, it appears to incorporate a space themed grid made up of connected circles (rather than more common hexagons or squares).  It appears players race across the grid, avoiding or destroying tie fighters along the way to the Death Star.  One space looks to be the "vent" in the Death Star that players are likely vying for.

This seems to be a either a UK or at least European release only.

Though named the same as Star Wars: Destroy Death Star Game, these are much different games.
A tile-based game published by The National Trust of Great Britain & Northern Ireland. Players construct period buildings from a pool of 100 tiles.

MagiFlux is a card game for 2-4 players. The inspiration for this game came from Fluxx and Magic:The Gathering.

Objective of the game is to be the first to have cards in play that have a total of 10 Victory Points.

At the beginning of each turn, a player takes the top card from his own library, and untaps all resources. During each turn, a player may play 1 card face down as a resource. Resources and cards played remain in play.To play a card, pay by tapping the number of resources equal to its cost. Each card's cost is also worth the same amount of Victory Points.

Player may activate the abilities on his cards in play. Ability costs must be paid or triggered before the ability is resolved. Costs are usually related to player's own cards. A player is out of the game if he has no cards in his library.

A simple roll and move game from probably the 1930's. It was produced in London, England and has a separate game board with 100 spaces, each numbered consecutively, and a small box that contains the game component. The box contains a die cup for rolling the 1 D6 die and 4 different colored plastic greyhound pieces. The rules are printed on the game box's inside cover.
 The game is for 2-4 players. You roll the die to move your dog around the game board. A six is needed to start the race and will also net you another turn during the game. As the dogs move on the board they can land on special spots that can aid or hinder the player's piece. Some spots allow another roll or to move ahead extra spaces. Some force you to lose a turn or move back. If your dog lands on another player's piece. The other player’s dog is sent back to the start. An exact roll is needed to win by landing on the 'Hare' space, number 100.

boardgame: Star Wars Electronic Laser Battle Game
description: This is a 2 player dexterity game where players are attempting to battle their way to the death star and destroy it.  Players have to test their reaction speeds in tie fighter battles.  The first one in each round to hit the correct weapon button, has one of 6 LEDs illuminated on the path to the Death Star in the middle.  Once there, they have the chance to destroy the Death Star if they can win a final battle.

Small children's coop game in which you try to save the dinosaurs from an erupting vulcano.

Dinosaur counters come in four distinct colors. They are mingled with some explorer counters and then put face down in the middle of the table.

The players take turns in rolling the die. When one of the colors turns up, the player throws a cardboard lasso, trying to catch one or more counters. Those are turned faced up and the ones matching the color of the die roll are considered rescued and taken out of the game. Explorer counters are simply taken out of the game. Non matching counters are turned face down again.

When a vulcano is thrown, one of five vulcano pieces is turned over. When all five pieces are turned over the game is lost. If players succeed in rescueing all dinosaurs before that happens, they win the game jointly.

WARNING: the Playmobil theme is a loose one and although the cover art suggests that you might expect to find some real dinosaur playing pieces in the box, this is a plain cardboard game.

Ekodetektyw was published in cooperation with NFOŚiGW (roughly: National Environment Protection and Water Economy Fund). Its goal is to educate children about what is harmful to the environment and what they can do to protect it. Players move their pieces in various directions to collect tokens. Pictures on the tokens are connected with pollution, global warming, endangered species, recycling etc. The aim is to collect more pairs of tokens than other players. The winner gains the title of 'great ecologist'.

"Journal Singapore is the first board game to feature the history of Singapore over a period of three eras(1900 - 1965). Take on the role of pioneers and leave a legacy of prestige and influence; or find yourself consigned to the dustbin of history. Struggle to survive and thrive in the face of foreign domination, drastic resource shortages and vicious infighting; work with other players to see the dawn of an independent nation (or trample them into the dirt).

Journal Singapore is an easy to learn strategic boardgame that is suitable for two kinds of play; play a single era for a fast game or play competitively throughout the three eras.

Players choose their favourite character and pit their wits (and character traits) against other players while trying to survive the obstacles and effects of real historical events."

Ozone is a 3 in-a-row game.

Players take turns placing blocks. When you place a block you can score any combos caused by that block's creation. A combo must include the block just placed, and consists of adjacent blocks when viewed from any of the 5 possible directions (like in Rumis or Pueblo) - the blocks must LOOK adjacent, not necessarily be adjacent. The rules variations differ only in which combos score.

There are rules for two different games - OZONE and 3NOZO. In OZONE they score for each OOO or O3 combo that they create themselves. In 3NOZO one player scores for OOO and O3 combos, while the other scores for NNN, N3, ZZZ, NZZ (Ns and Zs are functionally equivalent), but in this case they score for combos created by either player.

From the back of the box:

Planetary Empires is an expansion for Warhammer 40,000 that provides players with a plastic map-building kit.  Rules are included that allow players to carve out their own empire by fighting battles using the Warhammer 40,000 rules.
A trivia soccer game. When the CD is played, every player listens and tries to answer the questions. The questions areasked by Jack van Gelder. There are about 500 questions.
Roll a colour die and then use your piranha to try to catch the correct coloured fish. Catching the wrong coloured fish gives a penalty.

A CCG of Disney / Pixar released for the super market company Delhaize in Belgium. Each package includes 5 cards with the entire collection existing out of 216 cards.

The rules for the game are simple: Every player takes at least 5 cards face down. Now the first player takes the first card from his deck and chooses one of the four characteristics (friendship, courage, leadership or star quality) which all have a specific score. After choosing the category the player places the card on the table so that everybody can see it. Now the other players take the first card from their own deck and compare the score of the choosen characteristic. The player with the highest score on that characteristic wins the round. The game goes on until one person has won all the cards in the game.

For collecting purposes people have made their own interpretation of the game or let the winner choose a card from one of the losers decks.

Play the French disaster of disasters in three hours.

The objective is to make a fast-played and fundamentally simple campaign system that can be used for most wars of the 1700-1880 period. The system will focus on command and have a distinct "you are here" -feel. The system rules will be a maximum of 16 pages long including examples and notes and a lot of pictures.
Nothing is set in stone, yet, but the scale is something like 1500 meters per hex and three turns per day (morn, noon, night). Units will be divisions and brigades.
Combat happens in hex during movement. There will be no chits drawn out of cups, but there will be messengers carrying orders around on the map. There will be no rules with French names, but there will be a stress on the quirks of characters involved.

boardgame: Battlestations: Deep Ones in Deep Space
description:
This is an mini module for Battlestations, the board game of starship adventure.  It allows players to tackle Lovercraftian Horrors.

From the game itself:
"Adventures in the Rift.  Discover what stirs after the beacon at Far Innsmouth shines a new light in deepest, darkest space.

Bring Lovecraftian horror to your Battlestations universe.

New equipment, a new species (Eugenes), new special abilities and new actions (including the new skill: Sanity!)

This book includes over 2 dozen threaded missions with different finales and a collection of short stories written in the vein of Howard Phillips Lovecraft. "

TURNS
At each turn, each player moves a friendly stone.:
Stones move to one diagonally adjacent empty cell (forward or backward).
A stone is removed if it reaches the pyramid top (cell k14).

ATTACKS
There are no captures, but enemy stones can be delayed in two ways:
By contact: If a stone moves forwards to a cell adjacent to an enemy stone, then this stone moves diagonally backwards to an empty cell. If the enemy stone cannot retreat (because both cells are occupied) the actual player gets an extra move.
By column: if there are exactly one black and one white stone at a column, the higher stone can be attacked. If the other player moves one stone forward to that column, the upper stone is placed in an empty cell on the lowest row of that column (which is accessible by a sequence of moves: in a chess-like board, the game only uses black squares). If the enemy stone cannot retreat (it was at the fifth row) the current player gets an extra move.
If both attacks occur in a single move, column attacks takes precedence over contact attacks. That is, the player only executes the column retreat. If that retreat is not possible, he executes the contact retreat. If that retreat is also not possible, the player gets an extra move.

LAST STONE
If a player only has one stone, it may move one or two cells (mover's choice) and may retreat one or two cells after an attack by contact (attacker's choice).

GOAL
If a player repeats a position by the third time, he loses.

The Munchkin Kill-O-Meter allows you to track combat strength during any Munchkin game, including Munchkin Quest, which can be handy since it's easy to lose track of such things in the heat of battle. Each version of the Munchkin Kill-O-Meter includes a two-sided combat counter with which to track your current strength as well as two cards unique to that version. More specifically:


    The initial Munchkin Kill-O-Meter includes one card for the standard Munchkin card game and one card for use in Munchkin Quest.
    Munchkin Zombies Kill-O-Meter includes two cards themed for Munchkin Zombies, while also being usable in any Munchkin game.
    Munchkin Cthulhu Kill-O-Meter, as you might guess, includes two cards themed for Munchkin Cthulhu.
    Munchkin Apocalypse Kill-O-Meter includes eighty-seven, I mean, two cards themed for Munchkin Apocalypse.
    Munchkin Steampunk Kill-O-Meter comes packaged with, yes, two cards themed for Munchkin Steampunk.
    Munchkin Pathfinder Kill-O-Meter comes packaged with Four cards themed for Munchkin Pathfinder.
    Munchkin Warhammer 40,000 Kill-O-Meter comes packaged with Four cards themed for Munchkin Warhammer 40,000.
    Munchkin Warhammer Age of Sigmar Kill-O-Meter comes packaged with Four cards themed for Munchkin Warhammer Age of Sigmar.

Another Official Munchkin Plastic Bag!  Extra bonus -- free Munchkins!

These six female Munchkin pawns are scheduled to appear in a 2010 Munchkin Quest supplement . . . but if you want them early, here they are! You get one in each of the six Munchkin Quest player colors. The obvious thing to do would be to throw them into your Munchkin Quest box along with the ones that came with the game, and use the correct pawn for whatever sex you character happens to be at that moment.

But if you are truly evil -- and your friends let you get away with it -- you'll show the world just what a munchkin you really are by using the Munchkin Babes in other games.

In Munchkin

Any time you are entitled to go up a level -- or when you do something that WOULD earn you a level if you weren't already Level 9 -- you may, instead, place a munchkin pawn in front of you. In any combat, you may discard it for a 1d6 bonus. You may only have one in front of you at a time. Unless, of course, you "lose" these rules and tell the other players that you're allowed to have more. Six is a good number.

In Other Games

Because you're a munchkin, you may claim a munchkin advantage for using these pawns in ANY game. For instance:


    In any roll-and-move game, if you use the munchkin as your pawn rather than one that came with the game, then once per game you may add or subtract 1 space to the move you roll. 
    In any game in which you start with money, if you use the munchkin as your pawn you start with 10% extra. Round up, of course. 
    In chess, a munchkin pawn can move forward two spaces at any time. 


If you create your own official rules for using the Munchkin pawn in other games, please share your rules with us at the Steve Jackson Games forums.

Corporate Espionage is a card game that represents a battle between freshly-funded startup companies who all have similar ideas and are entering the same target market. Each company must ramp up quickly in order to compete... and when that fails, take out the competition by any means necessary.

     Drive competitors to bankruptcy with corporate espionage
     Last company standing wins!



Each deck contains 80 unique, illustrated cards.

--From the Publisher's website

boardgame: Western Desert Force: The Desert Campaign 1940-1942
description:
In September 1940, Italian divisions rolled over the border between their colony of Libya and British-dominated Egypt. After advancing several dozen miles they stopped to secure their supply lines, only to meet a devastating British counter-attack that soon had them reeling back halfway across Libya. For the next two and a half years, Axis and Allied armies surged back and forth across the North African desert until finally Gen. Bernard Law Montgomery's British Eighth Army wrecked Erwin Rommel's vaunted Panzer Army Africa.

Western Desert Force: The Desert Campaign 1940-1942 is a quick-playing game from the same designer as our Defiant Russia, following the same philosophy: a game you can play in just an hour or two, with the emphasis on tense action and lots of fun.

The map portrays the North Africa from El Agheila to Alexandria. It's divided into hexagons, at a scale of 15 miles across each.

Players control the actual units that fought in this campaign. Units are divisions, brigades and regiments with some battalions. The Axis player controls German, Italian and Italian Colonial (Libyan) units; the Allied player leads British, Australian, New Zealand, South African, Indian, Polish, Jewish, Free French and Greek units in the fight against fascism.

The objectives for each side are simple: kick the enemy out of North Africa. The strategy is not so easy: the Allies must hold on to protect the Suez Canal; the Axis must choke off this vital waterway. Neither can afford to be ejected from the otherwise worthless desert.

The game design is by William Sariego, and development is by industry veteran Paul Dangel in his Avalanche Press debut.

Western Desert Force includes:

     One 33"x17" map
     Full-color play aids
     140 game pieces
     16-page rulebook


Game Scale:
Game Turn: 1 day
Hex: 15 miles / 24 kilometers
Units: Battalion to Division

Game Inventory:
Three 11 x 17" full color maps
One dual-side printed countersheet (140 1/2" counters)
One 16-page Western Desert Force rulebook
Three single-side printed Player Aid cards
One dual-side printed Random Events Card
Two 6-sided dice

Solitaire Playability: High
Complexity Level: Medium
Players: 2 or more
Playing Time: 2-4 hours

A roll-and-move game in which the board is a map of the United States, with certain spaces designated with cities' radio stations. Players start from Station WEAF in New York and make a "complete circuit" around the country.

The game features an unusual mechanism for movement. Instead of a die or spinner, players use a "radio tuner" with two turning card discs. Shutter windows in the tuner reveal a station call sign (like WEAF) on one side and a number on the other. Players look up the station on the back of the tuner, where it is associated with a numerical value; that value is then added to the number revealed on the front. Therefore, if a player "tunes in" to Station WMC (Memphis), and the number 3, they then look on the back to see that WEC has a value of 1. That player then moves 3+1 spaces on the board.

If players land on the same station they "tuned in" on their turn, they get a free extra turn. Players must "tune in" an exact number of spaces to finish their route and end on WEAF back in New York.

3 game set featured the following titles:

Ambushed
Desert Towns

The movement of cars is regulated by a spinner and also by radio flashcards sent from Police Headwuarters to the Squad Cars. The spinner regulates movement. If all getaway cars are captured by the Squad Cars the police have won. If just one getaway car escapes then the bank robbers have won the game.

boardgame: Dorking 1875: The German Conquest of Britain
description: From the publisher "In 1871 a serving British officer called GT Chesney wrote a short story that sent shockwaves through Victorian Britain. Frustrated by the state of the army, he wrote "The Battle of Dorking", the story of Britain's invasion and total defeat at the hands of a newly-united Germany. It was to influence both literature and British army reform for years to come. "1875: The Battle of Dorking" allows two players to find out what might have happened at the key battle of this fictional campaign. Along with the rules and all the cut-outs needed, full background notes are given regarding Chesney's original book, the context of the campaign and the weaponry, organisation and tactics of both armies. A game should last two hours on a first play, reducing to 90 minutes once familiar with the rules."

description: A 64 page supplement for the pre-painted miniatures game AT-43 with background and rules for using the collectivist and revolutionary Oni army in the game. Adds complete details for the first new army not found in the original game. Printed in full color.

There are four suits, Hen, Ducks Goose, and Turkey.Each suit is numbered 1 to 10. There are four cards which are always trumps, no matter what the suit is:

    Chantecler (Rooster)
    Peacock
    Pheasant
    Owl


The persons sitting opposite are partners. Every player gets 11 cards. Each player then bids in privilege of naming the trump. The highest bidder decides the suit he/she selects for trump and all players discard down to 6 cards.
The player naming the trump then plays face up in the center of the table a card of the suit he/she has named, with as aim winning as many of the counter cards as possible.

Then the next player plays a card of the suit named. The player with the highest values takes the four cards.(called The Trick). After the first Trick is played its optional whether or not trumps are lead.

Scoring:

When the hand is finished, each side adds together the counter cards (1,3,5 and the trump cards) which they have taken and credit to their score. If a bidder fails to make as many points as he/she bid, he/she is said to be set, and the number bid is subtracted from his score.  The first side scoring 56 points wins the game.

description:
It has been 2 days since the zombie outbreak. A handful of cliché characters have managed to survive the onslaught by holing at the center of the town. But a rescue never comes. Worse still, the zombies are beginning to gather on the town. As their numbers grow, so does a strange intelligence in the undead hordes. Time grows short. If the characters wish to survive they have to do it themselves by escaping to the outskirts of the town.

In Posthumous, half the players will command a single cliche character apiece, while the other half direct hordes of themed zombies. The town's layout, characters' and zombies strengths and weaknesses are all randomized each game, making every play through a distinguished experience. While on the surface, Posthumous's humor celebrates the proud history of the Zombie Flick, the game is at its core one of cunning and tactical use of your entire team's abilities.

Posthumous's unique mechanics reduce downtime and keep the players plotting, laughing, and guessing. Games of up 10 players can expect their games to last no longer than games with only 4.

Roughly can be described as a monopoly-like game divided in 3 cycles (ciclos in Portuguese). On the first you struggle for inheritance and knowledge, struggling to find an career. On the second, you seek to buy land, invest in business, aiming to get money. On the final cycle you aim to overcome the challenges of life and become the richest player.

Translation of the publisher description:

"Evolve!
Strategy and Planning are essential to win this thrilling game.
Choose strategically the best moment to evolve and change to a new CYCLE (cycle = ciclo in portuguese)

1st CYCLE:
Win inheritance of the lands and acquire knowledge to graduate and choose a profession.
2nd CYCLE:
Buy land, invest in business, earn money and wealth and get ready to advance to the final CYCLE
3rd CYCLE:
Advance and overcome the challenges of LIFE and get to the end whit more money and wealth to be the great winner.

(...)

Our site:

www.jogociclos.com.br

With flawless quality CICLOS doesn't fall back when compared with the other boardgames currently in market.

(From the translator: please note that this game is being released in Brazil, where the boardgame market consists mainly of "War", a simplified version of "Risk", "Monopoly", "Clue" and "The Game of LIFE". Only recently a few new titles have been released, but, still, the closest we have to modern games were "Hart an der Grenze" (which has been banished from market), "Sultan" (both from Brazilian authors), "Go Bluffing", and a "Diamond" clone, as far as I recall)
